paper_title: Materials and Methods for Pollen Tube Ion Flux and Statistical Analysis

methods.description: This method involves measuring extracellular ion fluxes in elongating pollen tubes of Hyacinthus orientalis L. and Nicotiana tabacum L. using the ELoPvC 2 system. The method allows for non-invasive observation of ion fluxes over time, ensuring the system remains intact and grows freely during measurement.

steps.description: The measurements were performed on the elongating pollen tubes using ELoPvC 2, which made it possible to observe the extracellular ion fluxes in one time series.
steps.searchable_summary: use ELoPvC 2 to measure ion fluxes in pollen tubes
outputs: time series data of ion fluxes

steps.description: Samples were pre-incubated in a water bath shaker at 130 rpm for 1.5 h at room temperature in the dark.
steps.searchable_summary: pre-incubate pollen samples in water bath shaker

steps.description: The conductivity measurements of the germination medium were taken using a CC-105 conductivity meter.
A sample containing pollen tubes was placed in a germination medium and transferred onto a photovoltaic semiconductor plate located in a grounded Faraday cage.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer pollen tube sample to semiconductor plate

steps.description: The DC voltage was measured in the physiological temperature range using a DMM 4040 Precision Multimeter.

methods.method_name: Statistical Analysis of Time Series Data
methods.description: This method involves the analysis of time series data collected from pollen tube ion flux measurements using various statistical metrics and information theory techniques to understand the complex nature of the data.

steps.description: The series of time data collected at each temperature were first detrended.
The nonlinear statistical metrics, namely the Hurst exponent, the largest Lyapunov exponent, and the entropy of a measured time series, were evaluated.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Hurst exponent, Lyapunov exponent, and entropy

steps.description: The information entropy was calculated from the histogram data in R code at physiological temperatures.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate information entropy from histogram data

steps.description: The phase space was reconstructed using the Takens theorem.
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct phase space using Takens theorem

steps.description: The power spectral density (PSD) was calculated by the Welch method and covered with the results of the discrete Fourier transform.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate PSD using Welch method and DFT
